element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Collect to increase firepower, directional switching via B button
Stack up to 5 for enhanced fire pellet visuals and damage; excess grants bonus points
Select button activation; one stock per life; must be recollected after death
Screen stops scrolling; manage sustained enemy fire while targeting single boss

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Final section of each main level
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Comic book villains (Reptyl, Mephisto, Possessor, Fire Lord, Kylor) requiring multiple hits
Alternates with side-scrolling; changes spatial threat profile
Sends player to Magik Domain for Cosmic Device retrieval

element_introduction_order.element: Final boss (mysterious purple entity with handgun)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Likely Mister Sinister; different visual paradigm from previous encounters

typical_relief_triggers: Completing level section with mini-boss defeated
Collecting power-up drops after enemy waves
Reaching safe zone between scrolling sequences
typical_tension_triggers: Endless waves of tanks, monsters, mutants, ghosts, bats, robots
Mini-boss stationary encounters with sustained projectile patterns
Loss of all equipped spheres and firepower on death
Screen-wide threat density in overhead sections
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Relentless forward momentum punctuated by forced-stop boss battles; tension peaks at auto-scroll segments with dense enemy waves, releases briefly at boss defeat; difficulty-driven pacing creates anxiety-fatigue cycle characteristic of Nintendo Hard design

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate introduction of basic shooting and option collection; foundational enemy patterns establish core rhythm
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating enemy density and boss complexity; overhead perspective sections introduce spatial disorientation; power-up loss on death creates escalating resource pressure
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sustained high-difficulty gauntlets; super-villain encounters demand precision and pattern memorization; Magik Domain final section represents peak mechanical and spatial challenge
notable_spikes: First mini-boss encounter establishes intensity baseline
Transition to overhead perspective sections (spatial unfamiliarity)
Super-villain final sections with multi-hit requirements
Magik Domain final boss with non-standard attack pattern (handgun rather than energy)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level divides into distinct sections with mini-boss at section end; third section contains super-villain requiring sustained multi-hit engagement; screen stops scrolling during boss encounters allowing focused combat
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic main campaign levels (Reptyl, Mephisto, Possessor, Fire Lord, Kylor encounters)

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels mix side-scrolling and overhead viewpoints; overhead sections alter threat detection and movement constraints, increasing cognitive load and spatial disorientation
Continuous auto-scrolling through enemy gauntlets punctuated by forced mini-boss standoffs; no backtracking; unidirectional progression creates momentum-driven pacing
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Standard level sections between boss encounters

notable_level_patterns.description: After first level set completion, story trigger sends player to Magik Domain; introduces meta-boss (Galactus) as narrative/structural element; unlocks final mission and additional quests
